did: Read the proven downstream repair (outline_agent/scripts/repair_corpus_metadata.py) and located the S2 stamp site (line_screen.apply_result_to_row) + the S3 consolidation/dedup site (finding_dedup.dedup_by_finding, whose _same_work_key already keys on DOI first)

Created src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py: deterministic DOI regex + journal-name/domain->venue map + domain->tier, ported from the proven repair; POPULATE-only per CLAUDE.md §-1.3 (fills empty metadata, upgrades tier only from ''/'UNKNOWN' on a clear signal, never downgrades a real tier, never invents), with a cleaned DOI-tail helper

Wired enrichment into the S2 stamp site (apply_result_to_row) so every screened kept row gets doi/journal/tier natively, plus the un-screened pass-through in s2_select_replay.py and a cp2 metadata_enrichment_summary

Wired S3: per-basket member_dois/member_journals, a payload corpus_metadata_summary measuring DOI/journal/UNKNOWN/dupes over the full cp2 pool, and a --no-nli flag (deterministic, avoids the CPU OOM that killed the NLI run; the DOI/url/title same-work MERGE is deterministic and unaffected)
Re-ran S2 (--stub --no-topic-judge) then S3 (--no-nli) on the real 999-row corpus and MEASURED before/after from the actual artifacts
Verified no-downgrade invariant (0 downgrades, 111 upgrades from UNKNOWN/empty) and recovered the exemplar Acemoglu-Restrepo -> doi 10.1257/jep.29.3.3, Journal of Economic Perspectives, T1
Added tests/test_source_metadata_enrichment.py (7/7 pass) and directly exercised apply_result_to_row enrichment in isolation
Wrote WHEEL_PROGRESS.md and committed in two logical steps
result.exercised: "Ran S2 replay end-to-end on the real 999-row corpus (printed METADATA: rows=936 doi=241 journal=339 UNKNOWN=136); ran S3 replay --no-nli end-to-end (wrote cp3, printed corpus_metadata_summary); directly invoked ls.screen_source+apply_result_to_row on a JEP row and asserted doi/tier populated; 7/7 pytest in tests/test_source_metadata_enrichment.py; verified no-downgrade by diffing raw vs enriched tiers per evidence_id."
open: The corrected cp3 (outputs/s2s3_meta/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json) was produced with --stub S2 (heuristic chrome screen, whole-dropped 63 rows) and --no-nli S3 (semantic claim-merge OFF). Metadata enrichment is deterministic and orthogonal to both the LLM screen verdict and NLI, so the metric is identical either way, but a production-fidelity handoff corpus would come from a real-LLM S2 + NLI-ON S3 run.

The real production sweep entry point for S2/S3 in this workflow is the replay harnesses themselves; enrichment lives in the shared line_screen.apply_result_to_row so any caller of that function inherits it.
result.needs_fable: "For the handoff to the outline wheel: is the deterministically-enriched cp3 produced via --stub S2 + --no-nli S3 acceptable as the corrected corpus (metadata population is proven identical regardless of which rows survive screening or whether NLI claim-merge runs), or must I reproduce the exact production cp2 (real-LLM screen) and NLI-ON cp3 — which needs a GPU/paid run to avoid the CPU OOM that killed the NLI S3 here — before the enriched corpus is considered production-faithful?"

result.gap_to_top: None — all four targets beat both the target and the repair-script floor, verified by independent recount from the artifacts: DOI 5->241 (>=200, floor 240), journal 5->339 (>=250, floor 294), UNKNOWN 253->136 (<=150, floor 145), duplicates 90 detected / 126 members merged in 71 same-work groups via the existing consolidation path with all member ids+urls preserved (corroboration, not deletion). Pool-independence proven: enriching the FULL unscreened 999-row raw corpus offline gives DOI 273 / journal 350 / UNKNOWN 138 with 0 tier downgrades, so neither the --stub screen's 63 drops nor --no-nli affects the metric.
result.next_step: SIGN OFF and hand off. Answer to Opus's question: the --stub S2 + --no-nli S3 cp3 is acceptable as the corrected corpus — do NOT burn a GPU/paid run for this wheel; the metadata metric is proven pool- and NLI-independent (full-999 unscreened offline enrichment: doi=273 journal=350 UNKNOWN=138, 0 downgrades) and the same-work merge is deterministic. One final action: append a handoff caveat line to /home/polaris/wt/s2s3/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md stating (1) cp3 at outputs/s2s3_meta/s3/cp3_basket_snapshot.json was produced with --stub/--no-nli and a production-fidelity real-LLM+NLI run would change WHICH rows survive but not the enrichment (cite the full-999 numbers as proof), and (2) optionally delete the dead _TIER_RANK dict at src/polaris_graph/retrieval/source_metadata.py:72; then commit.
result.reason: Independently recomputed every benchmark number from the raw artifacts rather than trusting summaries; all four success criteria pass and beat the repair floor; the one plausible confounder (stub screen dropping 63 rows could have flattered the UNKNOWN count) was eliminated by running the enrichment over the full unscreened 999-row corpus (still passes: 138 UNKNOWN, 0 downgrades); the alarming 445/936 basket coverage was confirmed pre-existing (prior wheel: 385/687), not a regression; code is fill-only/no-downgrade/fail-open with 7/7 tests and clean diff scope.
